title: Wrangell under water boil advisory
summary: Wrangell’s much anticipated Water Treatment Plant is almost complete on Feb. 13, 2025. Raw water will run through pipes before treatment. title: Judge orders Trump administration to ‘stop violating the law!’ and publish spending details
summary: Office of Management and Budget Director Russ Vought testifies before the Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs Committee on Jan. 15, 2025. (Screenshot from committee webcast) WASHINGTON — A federal judge on Monday ordered the Trump administration to once again publish details about the pace at which it plans to spend money approved by Congress. U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia Judge Emmet Sullivan wrote in his ruling that Congress “has sweeping authority” to require the president to post a website detailing how it doles out taxpayer dollars throughout the year. “As explained in this Memorandum Opinion, there is nothing unco...

title: Crews continue mop up work on Tlozhavun and Turtle Fires; firefighters respond to and monitor additional fires in northeastern Alaska
summary: Update 6 p.m., July 21, 2025: Due to reduced wildland fire activity and successful suppression efforts on the Turtle Fire (#243) and other fires in the Ptarmigan Complex, the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) Eastern Interior Field Office lifted the fire closure order that temporarily closed the Lower Birch Creek Wayside near mile 140 of the Steese Highway. The closure ended today, leaving no remaining public access cl...

title: NEWS: Local Housing Planning Bill Becomes Law Without Governor’s Signature
summary: Anchorage, AK — Last week, Senate Bill 50 , sponsored by Senator Forrest Dunbar , D-Anchorage, which strengthens local housing planning across Alaska, officially became law without the Governor’s signature. The post NEWS: Local Housing Planning Bill Becomes Law Without Governor’s Signature appeared...

title: Mop-up continues as removing gear begins on the Goldrun Complex
summary: Cool wet weather is expected through Tuesday and will aid the firefighters of the Goldrun Complex . The Goldrun Complex, located in the Kobuk Valley, consists of nine fires that continue to smolder and show little fire activity. Back hauling, the process of removing unused and unnecessary equipment,...

title: AST SAR: Fairbanks [AK25071467]
summary: One 20 July at approximately 1700 hours, Alaska State Trooper in Fairbanks received a Garmin SOS alert for 4 boaters who were stranded on the Delta River. Helo 2 was dispatched the following day and recovered all 4 boaters and returned them safely to their vehicle.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: We Lost a Vote—But Not the Fight
summary: Watching the budget bill pass hurt. There’s no way around it. Despite everything we poured into this fight—the long nights, the relentless pressure, the stories shared, the ground gained—Congress has once again sold-out Alaska and our public lands to billionaires. In a budget deal led by President Trump and his allies, we saw sweeping new mandates for Arctic drilling (among a slew of reckless policies), reinf...

title: Backhaul and mop-up efforts underway as containment grows on the Nenana Ridge Complex
summary: Bonanza Creek Fire (#238): 12,559 acres, 15% containment Goldstream Creek Fire (#270): 20,483 acres, 21% containment Personnel: 374 Payette Suppression Module mops up on Division X/R of the Nenana Ridge Complex. The module has been working in the area for several days extinguishing hot spots and removing burnable materials from the western edge of the Bonanza Creek Fire. Photo by Payette SMOD Evacuation Information: Evacuation levels were downgraded Wednesday, July 16 th by the Fairbanks North Star Borough and the Alaska Division of Homeland Security and Emergency Management along the Parks Highway corridor. title: Monday, July 21, 2025
summary: Photo: Andrea Ivanoff, left, and Helen MacLean during a recent recording session for the Kenaitze Indian Tribe’s new Dena’ina language-learning app. (Hunter Morrison / KDLL) The Dena’ina people have lived on Alaska’s Kenai Peninsula for thousands of years, but today, the group’s Indigenous language isn’t spoken as widely as it once was. That’s why the Kenaitze Indian Tribe is launching interactive language-learning software...
